description: An immutable ledger entry recording every change to stock-on-hand for an item at a warehouse. Each GRN posts a RECEIPT that increments stockOnHand; an issue (consumption) posts an ISSUE that decrements it; a manual correction posts an ADJUSTMENT; a warehouse transfer posts a TRANSFER (debit on the source, credit on the destination). Movements are never deleted or edited; corrections post a new offsetting entry. This is the audit spine for inventory and the source for stock-on-hand reconstruction.

fields.name: id
fields.label: Movement ID
fields.purpose: Stable surrogate key for the ledger entry; ensures each movement is uniquely addressable for audit replay and reconciliation.

fields.name: itemId
fields.label: Item
fields.validation: must reference an existing Item; immutable
fields.purpose: Identifies which item's stock was moved; together with warehouseCode it locates the Inventory balance row to update.

fields.name: warehouseCode
fields.label: Warehouse
fields.validation: must reference an active Warehouse; immutable
fields.purpose: Identifies the warehouse where the movement occurred; drives the Inventory balance row updated and supports multi-warehouse stock-on-hand segregation.

fields.name: type
fields.label: Movement Type
fields.validation: one of RECEIPT (increments stockOnHand, posted by GRN) | ISSUE (decrements stockOnHand, posted by consumption) | ADJUSTMENT (manual correction by Inventory Manager, requires note) | TRANSFER (paired debit+credit across two warehouses)
fields.purpose: Classifies the reason for the stock change so reports can filter and aggregate by movement type (receipts vs consumption vs adjustments vs transfers) and the audit trail distinguishes automatic postings from manual corrections.

fields.name: quantity
fields.label: Quantity
fields.validation: > 0; in Item.stockUom; always positive regardless of movement direction (direction is determined by type; RECEIPT and TRANSFER-credit add; ISSUE and TRANSFER-debit subtract)
fields.purpose: The signed quantity of the stock change; always entered as a positive magnitude so the ledger arithmetic is type-driven, making replay and reconciliation unambiguous.

fields.name: reference
fields.label: Source Reference
fields.validation: free-text or a structured reference to the triggering document: e.g. grnId for RECEIPT, requisitionId for ISSUE, movementOrderId for TRANSFER; null for standalone ADJUSTMENTs without a source document
fields.purpose: Links the ledger entry back to the originating business event (GRN, requisition, transfer order) so full document-to-stock traceability is maintained and discrepancies can be investigated.

fields.name: at
fields.label: Movement Date/Time
fields.validation: now() when the movement is posted; immutable; the timestamp the ledger replay uses
fields.purpose: The authoritative timestamp of the stock change; drives chronological ledger replay, period-end closing balances, and the lastMovementAt field on the Inventory balance row.

fields.name: note
fields.label: Note
fields.mandatoryCondition: mandatory for type = ADJUSTMENT; optional for all other types
fields.validation: max 500 chars; mandatory for ADJUSTMENT to explain the correction reason
fields.purpose: Provides a human-readable explanation for manual adjustments so the audit trail records the business reason (e.g. physical count variance, damage write-off) rather than just the numeric change.

invariants: A21 a GRN posts a RECEIPT movement that increments stockOnHand on the matching Inventory row
A13 every movement is audited and broadcast; movements are immutable (no edits or deletes); corrections post a new offsetting ADJUSTMENT
